Parking is available nearby with a capacity of 448 cars.Jingu Administration Office "[http://www.isejingu.or.jp/mobile/i/parking/index.html Parking Information]" (accessed September 15, 2013) ; From Gekū to Yamasue Shrine From Gekū, head south along Mikumoto Road (御木本道路) adjacent to the Jingu Forest, turn left at the site of the former Toyomiya-zaki Bunko (豊宮崎文庫), and ascend moss-covered stone steps to reach {{ill|Watarai Okunitama-hime Shrine|ja|度会大国玉比賣神社}}, {{ill|Igari Shrine|ja|伊我理神社}}, and {{ill|Inaka Shrine|ja|井中神社}}.Ise Bunka Publishing (2008): pp.37-38 Continuing approximately 500 meters further along the forest will lead to Yamasue Shrine. == References == {{Reflist}} == 参考文献 == * 宇治山田市役所 編『宇治山田市史 上巻』宇治山田市役所、昭和4th year 1月20日、862p. * [[学研パブリッシング]]『伊勢神宮に行こう』Gakken Mook神社紀行セレクションvol.1、[[薗田稔]]監修、学研マーケティング、2013rd year 7月4日、82p. ISBN 978-4-05-610047-1 * 式内社研究会 編『式内社調査報告 第六巻 東海道1』[[Kogakkan University]]出版部、平成2nd year 2月28日、690p. ISBN 4-87644-080-8 * 『お伊勢さん125社めぐり』別冊『伊勢人』、[[伊勢文化舎]]、平成20th year 12月23日、151p. ISBN 978-4-900759-37-4 == See Also == * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|ja|伊勢国の式内社一覧}} * {{ill|List of 125 Shrines of Ise|ja|神宮125社の一覧|lt=List of 125 Shrines of Ise|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|3=伊勢国の式内社一覧}} == External Links == * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m02/0216.html 山末神社（やまずえじんじゃ）] - 財団法人伊勢神宮崇敬会 {{神道 横}} {{125 Shrines of Ise}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Branch shrines of Ise Grand Shrine]] [[Category:Shrines in Mie Prefecture]] [[Category:山末神社]] [[Category:伊勢国の式内社]] [[Category:摂末社]] [[Category:伊勢市の神社]] [[Category:refreshing imported pages]] [[Category:bump 1-26]] [[Category:category refresh]] [[Category:125 Shrines of Ise]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category: tier 4]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:return the see also]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Yamazue Shrine]][[Category:qq]] }} [[ja:山末神社]] [[sn:Yamazue Shrine]] [[en:Yamazue Shrine]] [[sn:amazue Shrine]]

{{神社 |名称= 山末神社 | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image= [[File:Yamazue Shrine.jpg|270px]] |所在地= [[三重県]][[伊勢市]][[豊川町 (伊勢市)|豊川町]] |緯度度 = 34 | 緯度分 = 28 | 緯度秒 = 53.1 |経度度 = 136 |経度分 = 42 | 経度秒 = 21.5 |地図= Japan Mie |祭神= 大山津姫命 |社格= [[式内社]]（小）
{{ill|Toyouke Daijingu|ja|豊受大神宮|1=Toyouke Daijingu|2=ja|3=豊受大神宮|4=zh|5=豐受大神宮}}摂社 |創建= [[延暦]]23rd year （[[804]]）以前 |本殿= [[神明造]] |別名= |札所等= |例祭= |神事= [[祈年祭]]、[[月次祭]]ほか }} '''Yamazue Shrine''' (山末神社) is a {{ill|branch shrine|ja|摂末社}} of {{ill|Toyouke Daijingu|ja|豊受大神宮|1=Toyouke Daijingu|2=ja|3=豊受大神宮|4=zh|5=豐受大神宮}} (Outer Shrine) within the {{ill|Ise Grand Shrine|ja|伊勢神宮}} complex. It holds the 13th position among the 16 branch shrines of the Outer Shrine.Ujiyamada City Office, ed. (1929), pp. 9, 12. == Overview == Located within the precincts of the Outer Shrine in {{ill|Toyokawa, Ise|ja|豊川町 (伊勢市)}}, {{ill|Ise, Mie|ja|伊勢市}}, {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県}}.Compiled by Ujiyamada City Hall (1929): p.12 It is situated at the southeastern edge of {{ill|Mount Takakura (Ise)|ja|高倉山 (伊勢市)}} within the shrine grounds.Gakken Publishing (2013): p.59 However, it is not directly connected to the Outer Shrine’s main approach path, requiring visitors to exit the precinct before entering again. Further south of {{ill|Yamazue Shrine|ja|山末神社}} are the {{ill|Tanoe-Ōmizu Shrine|ja|田上大水神社・田上大水御前神社}}.Ise Bunka-sha (2008): p.38 The enshrined deity is Ōyama-tsu-hime no Mikoto (大山津姫命), revered as the mountain deity ({{ill|Yama-no-kami|ja|山の神}}) of the Outer Shrine’s sacred rice fields and the deity of the spring that irrigates them. These sacred rice fields were once known as "Toyomiyasaki no Mita," sprawling across the area south of Yamasue Shrine, though the land has since been urbanized. In *Ise Province Shrine Name Register Commentary* by Watarai Nobunori, the enshrined deity is referred to as "Ōyamakui no Kami, also known as Yamasue no Ōnushi no Kami." Ōyamakui no Kami (大山咋神) appears in the *{{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}}*, symbolizing the deification of sacred mountains.Compiled by the Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): p.349 The shrine structure is of {{ill|Shinmei-zukuri|ja|神明造}} style with a {{ill|ita-buki|ja|板葺}} (wooden shingle) roof, surrounded by a {{ill|Tamagaki|ja|玉垣}} (sacred fence).Compiled by the Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): p.351 A single {{ill|Shinmei torii|ja|神明鳥居}} stands, measuring 8 shaku 3 sun (approximately 2.5 meters) in height. Interestingly, the torii does not face the shrine’s approach but is positioned toward a stone wall. No offering box ({{ill|saisenbako|ja|賽銭箱}}) is provided. == History == The name "山末神社" (Yamasue Shrine) refers to its location at the end (末) of Mt. Takakura, where "末" indicates the summit rather than the base of the mountain. In documents such as the "{{ill|Engi Ise Daijingu-shiki|ja|延喜伊勢太神宮式}}" and the "{{ill|Toyuke Daijingu Gishikicho|ja|止由気宮儀式帳}}", it is written as "山末社". Based on the definition of auxiliary shrines of Ise Grand Shrine, it was established before the compilation of the "{{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳}}" in 927 (Enchō 5). Additionally, since it is mentioned in the "Toyuke Daijingu Gishikicho," it must have existed prior to 804 (Enryaku 23). Although "{{ill|Yamatohime-no-mikoto|ja|倭姫命世記|1=Yamatohime no Mikoto Seiki}}" does not directly mention the founding of Yamasue Shrine, it describes how Yamatohime-no-mikoto viewed the area from Yamasue while searching for a suitable location for the shrine. Rituals ceased by the end of the medieval period, and the shrine's location became unknown during the early modern period. However, through the efforts of the Chief Priest {{ill|Kawabe Kiyoshige|ja|河辺精長}}, the shrine was restored at its current site on June 23, 1663 (Kanbun 3, Julian calendar: July 27, 1663).Shikinaisha Kenkyukai (1990): p.350 During its restoration, references from "{{ill|Shinmei Hisho|ja|神名秘書}}" indicating "Yamasue Shrine, located in Miyama, Ohnashi Valley, south of Mitaguchi Shrine, Tsugihashi-go" were consulted for field surveys. The shrine was reconstructed on January 1, 1921 (Taisho 10), and again in July 1957 (Showa 32), with major renovations carried out on October 27, 1983 (Showa 58). == Rituals == The following rituals are performed by a group of three priests: Gon-negi, Miyakujou, and Shutsushi, in a巡回祭典 (巡回 ritual) format: * {{ill|Kinen-sai|ja|祈年祭}} (February 20) * {{ill|Tsukinami-sai|ja|月次祭|1=Tsukinami-no-Matsuri}} (June 20 and December 20) * {{ill|Kannamesai|ja|神嘗祭}} (October 20) * {{ill|Niinamesai|ja|新嘗祭}} (November 26) Remote rituals (遥祀) are conducted for: * {{ill|Saitan-sai|ja|歳旦祭}} (January 1) * {{ill|Genshi-sai|ja|元始祭}} (January 3) * National Foundation Day Festival (February 11) * {{ill|Kaze-no-Hi-no-Matsuri|ja|風日祈祭}} (May 14 and August 4) * {{ill|Tenchō-sai|ja|天長祭}} (December 23)Shikinaisha Kenkyukai (1990): p.352 == Access == ; To Gekū (Outer Shrine) * **By Train:** Approximately 5-minute walk from JR {{ill|Sangu Line|ja|参宮線}} or Kintetsu {{ill|Yamada Line|ja|近鉄山田線}} Ise-shi Station (south exit, JR side) via Gekū Sando.Ise Bunka Publishing (2008): p.33 * **By Bus:** About 5 minutes on foot from Mie Kotsu's "Ise-shi Station" bus stop. * **By Car:** About 5 minutes north from Ise-Nishi IC on the Ise Expressway via Mie Prefectural Route 32. Parking is available nearby with a capacity of 448 cars.Jingu Administration Office "[http://www.isejingu.or.jp/mobile/i/parking/index.html Parking Information]" (accessed September 15, 2013) ; From Gekū to Yamasue Shrine From Gekū, head south along Mikumoto Road (御木本道路) adjacent to the Jingu Forest, turn left at the site of the former Toyomiya-zaki Bunko (豊宮崎文庫), and ascend moss-covered stone steps to reach {{ill|Watarai Okunitama-hime Shrine|ja|度会大国玉比賣神社}}, {{ill|Igari Shrine|ja|伊我理神社}}, and {{ill|Inaka Shrine|ja|井中神社}}.Ise Bunka Publishing (2008): pp.37-38 Continuing approximately 500 meters further along the forest will lead to Yamasue Shrine. == References == {{Reflist}} == 参考文献 == * 宇治山田市役所 編『宇治山田市史 上巻』宇治山田市役所、昭和4th year 1月20日、862p. * [[学研パブリッシング]]『伊勢神宮に行こう』Gakken Mook神社紀行セレクションvol.1、[[薗田稔]]監修、学研マーケティング、2013rd year 7月4日、82p. ISBN 978-4-05-610047-1 * 式内社研究会 編『式内社調査報告 第六巻 東海道1』[[Kogakkan University]]出版部、平成2nd year 2月28日、690p. ISBN 4-87644-080-8 * 『お伊勢さん125社めぐり』別冊『伊勢人』、[[伊勢文化舎]]、平成20th year 12月23日、151p. ISBN 978-4-900759-37-4 == See Also == * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|ja|伊勢国の式内社一覧}} * {{ill|List of 125 Shrines of Ise|ja|神宮125社の一覧|lt=List of 125 Shrines of Ise|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|3=伊勢国の式内社一覧}} == External Links == * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m02/0216.html 山末神社（やまずえじんじゃ）] - 財団法人伊勢神宮崇敬会 {{神道 横}} {{125 Shrines of Ise}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Branch shrines of Ise Grand Shrine]] [[Category:Shrines in Mie Prefecture]] [[Category:山末神社]] [[Category:伊勢国の式内社]] [[Category:摂末社]] [[Category:伊勢市の神社]] [[Category:refreshing imported pages]] [[Category:bump 1-26]] [[Category:category refresh]] [[Category:125 Shrines of Ise]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:return the see also]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Yamazue Shrine]][[Category:qq]] }} [[ja:山末神社]] [[sn:Yamazue Shrine]] [[en:Yamazue Shrine]] [[sn:amazue Shrine]]

{{神社 |名称= 山末神社 | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image= [[File:Yamazue Shrine.jpg|270px]] |所在地= [[三重県]][[伊勢市]][[豊川町 (伊勢市)|豊川町]] |緯度度 = 34 | 緯度分 = 28 | 緯度秒 = 53.1 |経度度 = 136 |経度分 = 42 | 経度秒 = 21.5 |地図= Japan Mie |祭神= 大山津姫命 |社格= [[式内社]]（小）
{{ill|Toyouke Daijingu|ja|豊受大神宮|1=Toyouke Daijingu|2=ja|3=豊受大神宮|4=zh|5=豐受大神宮|12=draft}}摂社 |創建= [[延暦]]23rd year （[[804]]）以前 |本殿= [[神明造]] |別名= |札所等= |例祭= |神事= [[祈年祭]]、[[月次祭]]ほか }} '''Yamazue Shrine''' (山末神社) is a {{ill|branch shrine|ja|摂末社|12=draft}} of {{ill|Toyouke Daijingu|ja|豊受大神宮|1=Toyouke Daijingu|2=ja|3=豊受大神宮|4=zh|5=豐受大神宮|12=draft}} (Outer Shrine) within the {{ill|Ise Grand Shrine|ja|伊勢神宮|12=draft}} complex. It holds the 13th position among the 16 branch shrines of the Outer Shrine.Ujiyamada City Office, ed. (1929), pp. 9, 12. == Overview == Located within the precincts of the Outer Shrine in {{ill|Toyokawa, Ise|ja|豊川町 (伊勢市)|12=draft}}, {{ill|Ise, Mie|ja|伊勢市|12=draft}}, {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県|12=draft}}.Compiled by Ujiyamada City Hall (1929): p.12 It is situated at the southeastern edge of {{ill|Mount Takakura (Ise)|ja|高倉山 (伊勢市)|12=draft}} within the shrine grounds.Gakken Publishing (2013): p.59 However, it is not directly connected to the Outer Shrine’s main approach path, requiring visitors to exit the precinct before entering again. Further south of {{ill|Yamazue Shrine|ja|山末神社|12=draft}} are the {{ill|Tanoe-Ōmizu Shrine|ja|田上大水神社・田上大水御前神社|12=draft}}.Ise Bunka-sha (2008): p.38 The enshrined deity is Ōyama-tsu-hime no Mikoto (大山津姫命), revered as the mountain deity ({{ill|Yama-no-kami|ja|山の神|12=draft}}) of the Outer Shrine’s sacred rice fields and the deity of the spring that irrigates them. These sacred rice fields were once known as "Toyomiyasaki no Mita," sprawling across the area south of Yamasue Shrine, though the land has since been urbanized. In *Ise Province Shrine Name Register Commentary* by Watarai Nobunori, the enshrined deity is referred to as "Ōyamakui no Kami, also known as Yamasue no Ōnushi no Kami." Ōyamakui no Kami (大山咋神) appears in the *{{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記|12=draft}}*, symbolizing the deification of sacred mountains.Compiled by the Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): p.349 The shrine structure is of {{ill|Shinmei-zukuri|ja|神明造|12=draft}} style with a {{ill|ita-buki|ja|板葺|12=draft}} (wooden shingle) roof, surrounded by a {{ill|Tamagaki|ja|玉垣|12=draft}} (sacred fence).Compiled by the Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): p.351 A single {{ill|Shinmei torii|ja|神明鳥居|12=draft}} stands, measuring 8 shaku 3 sun (approximately 2.5 meters) in height. Interestingly, the torii does not face the shrine’s approach but is positioned toward a stone wall. No offering box ({{ill|saisenbako|ja|賽銭箱|12=draft}}) is provided. == History == The name "山末神社" (Yamasue Shrine) refers to its location at the end (末) of Mt. Takakura, where "末" indicates the summit rather than the base of the mountain. In documents such as the "{{ill|Engi Ise Daijingu-shiki|ja|延喜伊勢太神宮式|12=draft}}" and the "{{ill|Toyuke Daijingu Gishikicho|ja|止由気宮儀式帳|12=draft}}", it is written as "山末社". Based on the definition of auxiliary shrines of Ise Grand Shrine, it was established before the compilation of the "{{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|12=draft}}" in 927 (Enchō 5). Additionally, since it is mentioned in the "Toyuke Daijingu Gishikicho," it must have existed prior to 804 (Enryaku 23). Although "{{ill|Yamatohime-no-mikoto|ja|倭姫命世記|1=Yamatohime no Mikoto Seiki|12=draft}}" does not directly mention the founding of Yamasue Shrine, it describes how Yamatohime-no-mikoto viewed the area from Yamasue while searching for a suitable location for the shrine. Rituals ceased by the end of the medieval period, and the shrine's location became unknown during the early modern period. However, through the efforts of the Chief Priest {{ill|Kawabe Kiyoshige|ja|河辺精長|12=draft}}, the shrine was restored at its current site on June 23, 1663 (Kanbun 3, Julian calendar: July 27, 1663).Shikinaisha Kenkyukai (1990): p.350 During its restoration, references from "{{ill|Shinmei Hisho|ja|神名秘書|12=draft}}" indicating "Yamasue Shrine, located in Miyama, Ohnashi Valley, south of Mitaguchi Shrine, Tsugihashi-go" were consulted for field surveys. The shrine was reconstructed on January 1, 1921 (Taisho 10), and again in July 1957 (Showa 32), with major renovations carried out on October 27, 1983 (Showa 58). == Rituals == The following rituals are performed by a group of three priests: Gon-negi, Miyakujou, and Shutsushi, in a巡回祭典 (巡回 ritual) format: * {{ill|Kinen-sai|ja|祈年祭|12=draft}} (February 20) * {{ill|Tsukinami-sai|ja|月次祭|1=Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|12=draft}} (June 20 and December 20) * {{ill|Kannamesai|ja|神嘗祭|12=draft}} (October 20) * {{ill|Niinamesai|ja|新嘗祭|12=draft}} (November 26) Remote rituals (遥祀) are conducted for: * {{ill|Saitan-sai|ja|歳旦祭|12=draft}} (January 1) * {{ill|Genshi-sai|ja|元始祭|12=draft}} (January 3) * National Foundation Day Festival (February 11) * {{ill|Kaze-no-Hi-no-Matsuri|ja|風日祈祭|12=draft}} (May 14 and August 4) * {{ill|Tenchō-sai|ja|天長祭|12=draft}} (December 23)Shikinaisha Kenkyukai (1990): p.352 == Access == ; To Gekū (Outer Shrine) * **By Train:** Approximately 5-minute walk from JR {{ill|Sangu Line|ja|参宮線|12=draft}} or Kintetsu {{ill|Yamada Line|ja|近鉄山田線|12=draft}} Ise-shi Station (south exit, JR side) via Gekū Sando.Ise Bunka Publishing (2008): p.33 * **By Bus:** About 5 minutes on foot from Mie Kotsu's "Ise-shi Station" bus stop. * **By Car:** About 5 minutes north from Ise-Nishi IC on the Ise Expressway via Mie Prefectural Route 32. Parking is available nearby with a capacity of 448 cars.Jingu Administration Office "[http://www.isejingu.or.jp/mobile/i/parking/index.html Parking Information]" (accessed September 15, 2013) ; From Gekū to Yamasue Shrine From Gekū, head south along Mikumoto Road (御木本道路) adjacent to the Jingu Forest, turn left at the site of the former Toyomiya-zaki Bunko (豊宮崎文庫), and ascend moss-covered stone steps to reach {{ill|Watarai Okunitama-hime Shrine|ja|度会大国玉比賣神社|12=draft}}, {{ill|Igari Shrine|ja|伊我理神社|12=draft}}, and {{ill|Inaka Shrine|ja|井中神社|12=draft}}.Ise Bunka Publishing (2008): pp.37-38 Continuing approximately 500 meters further along the forest will lead to Yamasue Shrine. == References == {{Reflist}} == 参考文献 == * 宇治山田市役所 編『宇治山田市史 上巻』宇治山田市役所、昭和4th year 1月20日、862p. * [[学研パブリッシング]]『伊勢神宮に行こう』Gakken Mook神社紀行セレクションvol.1、[[薗田稔]]監修、学研マーケティング、2013rd year 7月4日、82p. ISBN 978-4-05-610047-1 * 式内社研究会 編『式内社調査報告 第六巻 東海道1』[[Kogakkan University]]出版部、平成2nd year 2月28日、690p. ISBN 4-87644-080-8 * 『お伊勢さん125社めぐり』別冊『伊勢人』、[[伊勢文化舎]]、平成20th year 12月23日、151p. ISBN 978-4-900759-37-4 == See Also == * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|ja|伊勢国の式内社一覧|12=draft}} * {{ill|List of 125 Shrines of Ise|ja|神宮125社の一覧|lt=List of 125 Shrines of Ise|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|3=伊勢国の式内社一覧|12=draft}} == External Links == * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m02/0216.html 山末神社（やまずえじんじゃ）] - 財団法人伊勢神宮崇敬会 {{神道 横}} {{125 Shrines of Ise}} {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:Branch shrines of Ise Grand Shrine]] [[Category:Shrines in Mie Prefecture]] [[Category:山末神社]] [[Category:伊勢国の式内社]] [[Category:摂末社]] [[Category:伊勢市の神社]] [[Category:refreshing imported pages]] [[Category:bump 1-26]] [[Category:category refresh]] [[Category:125 Shrines of Ise]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:return the see also]] }} [[sn:Yamazue Shrine]] [[ja:山末神社]] [[en:Yamazue Shrine]] [[sn:amazue Shrine]]

The shrine was reconstructed on January 1, 1921 (Taisho 10), and again in July 1957 (Showa 32), with major renovations carried out on October 27, 1983 (Showa 58). == Rituals == The following rituals are performed by a group of three priests: Gon-negi, Miyakujou, and Shutsushi, in a巡回祭典 (巡回 ritual) format: * {{ill|Kinen-sai|ja|祈年祭|12=draft}} (February 20) * {{ill|Tsukinami-sai|ja|月次祭|1=Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|12=draft}} (June 20 and December 20) * {{ill|Kannamesai|ja|神嘗祭|12=draft}} (October 20) * {{ill|Niinamesai|ja|新嘗祭|12=draft}} (November 26) Remote rituals (遥祀) are conducted for: * {{ill|Saitan-sai|ja|歳旦祭|12=draft}} (January 1) * {{ill|Genshi-sai|ja|元始祭|12=draft}} (January 3) * National Foundation Day Festival (February 11) * {{ill|Kaze-no-Hi-no-Matsuri|ja|風日祈祭|12=draft}} (May 14 and August 4) * {{ill|Tenchō-sai|ja|天長祭|12=draft}} (December 23)Shikinaisha Kenkyukai (1990): p.352 == Access == ; To Gekū (Outer Shrine) * **By Train:** Approximately 5-minute walk from JR {{ill|Sangu Line|ja|参宮線|12=draft}} or Kintetsu {{ill|Yamada Line|ja|近鉄山田線|12=draft}} Ise-shi Station (south exit, JR side) via Gekū Sando.Ise Bunka Publishing (2008): p.33 * **By Bus:** About 5 minutes on foot from Mie Kotsu's "Ise-shi Station" bus stop. * **By Car:** About 5 minutes north from Ise-Nishi IC on the Ise Expressway via Mie Prefectural Route 32. Parking is available nearby with a capacity of 448 cars.Jingu Administration Office "[http://www.isejingu.or.jp/mobile/i/parking/index.html Parking Information]" (accessed September 15, 2013) ; From Gekū to Yamasue Shrine From Gekū, head south along Mikumoto Road (御木本道路) adjacent to the Jingu Forest, turn left at the site of the former Toyomiya-zaki Bunko (豊宮崎文庫), and ascend moss-covered stone steps to reach {{ill|Watarai Okunitama-hime Shrine|ja|度会大国玉比賣神社|12=draft}}, {{ill|Igari Shrine|ja|伊我理神社|12=draft}}, and {{ill|Inaka Shrine|ja|井中神社|12=draft}}.Ise Bunka Publishing (2008): pp.37-38 Continuing approximately 500 meters further along the forest will lead to Yamasue Shrine. == References == {{Reflist}} == 参考文献 == * 宇治山田市役所 編『宇治山田市史 上巻』宇治山田市役所、昭和4th year 1月20日、862p. * [[学研パブリッシング]]『伊勢神宮に行こう』Gakken Mook神社紀行セレクションvol.1、[[薗田稔]]監修、学研マーケティング、2013rd year 7月4日、82p. ISBN 978-4-05-610047-1 * 式内社研究会 編『式内社調査報告 第六巻 東海道1』[[Kogakkan University]]出版部、平成2nd year 2月28日、690p. ISBN 4-87644-080-8 * 『お伊勢さん125社めぐり』別冊『伊勢人』、[[伊勢文化舎]]、平成20th year 12月23日、151p. ISBN 978-4-900759-37-4 == See Also == * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|ja|伊勢国の式内社一覧|12=draft}} * {{ill|List of 125 Shrines of Ise|ja|神宮125社の一覧|lt=List of 125 Shrines of Ise|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|3=伊勢国の式内社一覧|12=draft}} == External Links == * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m02/0216.html 山末神社（やまずえじんじゃ）] - 財団法人伊勢神宮崇敬会 {{神道 横}} {{125 Shrines of Ise}} {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:Branch shrines of Ise Grand Shrine]] [[Category:Shrines in Mie Prefecture]] [[Category:山末神社]] [[Category:伊勢国の式内社]] [[Category:摂末社]] [[Category:伊勢市の神社]] [[Category:refreshing imported pages]] [[Category:bump 1-26]] [[Category:category refresh]] [[Category:125 Shrines of Ise]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:return the see also]] }} [[sn:Yamazue Shrine]] [[ja:山末神社]] [[en:Yamazue Shrine]] [[sn:amazue Shrine]]

{{神社 |名称= 山末神社 | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image= [[File:Yamazue Shrine.jpg|270px]] |所在地= [[三重県]][[伊勢市]][[豊川町 (伊勢市)|豊川町]] |緯度度 = 34 | 緯度分 = 28 | 緯度秒 = 53.1 |経度度 = 136 |経度分 = 42 | 経度秒 = 21.5 |地図= Japan Mie |祭神= 大山津姫命 |社格= [[式内社]]（小）
{{ill|Toyouke Daijingu|ja|豊受大神宮|1=Toyouke Daijingu|2=ja|3=豊受大神宮|4=zh|5=豐受大神宮|12=draft}}摂社 |創建= [[延暦]]23rd year （[[804]]）以前 |本殿= [[神明造]] |別名= |札所等= |例祭= |神事= [[祈年祭]]、[[月次祭]]ほか }} '''Yamazue Shrine''' (山末神社) is a {{ill|branch shrine|ja|摂末社|12=draft}} of {{ill|Toyouke Daijingu|ja|豊受大神宮|1=Toyouke Daijingu|2=ja|3=豊受大神宮|4=zh|5=豐受大神宮|12=draft}} (Outer Shrine) within the {{ill|Ise Grand Shrine|ja|伊勢神宮|12=draft}} complex. It holds the 13th position among the 16 branch shrines of the Outer Shrine.Ujiyamada City Office, ed. (1929), pp. 9, 12. == Overview == Located within the precincts of the Outer Shrine in {{ill|Toyokawa, Ise|ja|豊川町 (伊勢市)|12=draft}}, {{ill|Ise, Mie|ja|伊勢市|12=draft}}, {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県|12=draft}}.Compiled by Ujiyamada City Hall (1929): p.12 It is situated at the southeastern edge of {{ill|Mount Takakura (Ise)|ja|高倉山 (伊勢市)|12=draft}} within the shrine grounds.Gakken Publishing (2013): p.59 However, it is not directly connected to the Outer Shrine’s main approach path, requiring visitors to exit the precinct before entering again. Further south of {{ill|Yamazue Shrine|ja|山末神社|12=draft}} are the {{ill|Tanoe-Ōmizu Shrine|ja|田上大水神社・田上大水御前神社|12=draft}}.Ise Bunka-sha (2008): p.38 The enshrined deity is Ōyama-tsu-hime no Mikoto (大山津姫命), revered as the mountain deity ({{ill|Yama-no-kami|ja|山の神|12=draft}}) of the Outer Shrine’s sacred rice fields and the deity of the spring that irrigates them. These sacred rice fields were once known as "Toyomiyasaki no Mita," sprawling across the area south of Yamasue Shrine, though the land has since been urbanized. In *Ise Province Shrine Name Register Commentary* by Watarai Nobunori, the enshrined deity is referred to as "Ōyamakui no Kami, also known as Yamasue no Ōnushi no Kami." Ōyamakui no Kami (大山咋神) appears in the *{{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記|12=draft}}*, symbolizing the deification of sacred mountains.Compiled by the Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): p.349 The shrine structure is of {{ill|Shinmei-zukuri|ja|神明造|12=draft}} style with a {{ill|ita-buki|ja|板葺|12=draft}} (wooden shingle) roof, surrounded by a {{ill|Tamagaki|ja|玉垣|12=draft}} (sacred fence).Compiled by the Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): p.351 A single {{ill|Shinmei torii|ja|神明鳥居|12=draft}} stands, measuring 8 shaku 3 sun (approximately 2.5 meters) in height. Interestingly, the torii does not face the shrine’s approach but is positioned toward a stone wall. No offering box ({{ill|saisenbako|ja|賽銭箱|12=draft}}) is provided. == History == The name "山末神社" (Yamasue Shrine) refers to its location at the end (末) of Mt. Takakura, where "末" indicates the summit rather than the base of the mountain. In documents such as the "{{ill|Engi Ise Daijingu-shiki|ja|延喜伊勢太神宮式|12=draft}}" and the "{{ill|Toyuke Daijingu Gishikicho|ja|止由気宮儀式帳|12=draft}}", it is written as "山末社". Based on the definition of auxiliary shrines of Ise Grand Shrine, it was established before the compilation of the "{{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|12=draft}}" in 927 (Enchō 5). Additionally, since it is mentioned in the "Toyuke Daijingu Gishikicho," it must have existed prior to 804 (Enryaku 23). Although "{{ill|Yamatohime-no-mikoto|ja|倭姫命世記|1=Yamatohime no Mikoto Seiki|12=draft}}" does not directly mention the founding of Yamasue Shrine, it describes how Yamatohime-no-mikoto viewed the area from Yamasue while searching for a suitable location for the shrine. Rituals ceased by the end of the medieval period, and the shrine's location became unknown during the early modern period. However, through the efforts of the Chief Priest {{ill|Kawabe Kiyoshige|ja|河辺精長|12=draft}}, the shrine was restored at its current site on June 23, 1663 (Kanbun 3, Julian calendar: July 27, 1663).Shikinaisha Kenkyukai (1990): p.350 During its restoration, references from "{{ill|Shinmei Hisho|ja|神名秘書|12=draft}}" indicating "Yamasue Shrine, located in Miyama, Ohnashi Valley, south of Mitaguchi Shrine, Tsugihashi-go" were consulted for field surveys. The shrine was reconstructed on January 1, 1921 (Taisho 10), and again in July 1957 (Showa 32), with major renovations carried out on October 27, 1983 (Showa 58). == Rituals == The following rituals are performed by a group of three priests: Gon-negi, Miyakujou, and Shutsushi, in a巡回祭典 (巡回 ritual) format: * {{ill|Kinen-sai|ja|祈年祭|12=draft}} (February 20) * {{ill|Tsukinami-sai|ja|月次祭|1=Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|12=draft}} (June 20 and December 20) * {{ill|Kannamesai|ja|神嘗祭|12=draft}} (October 20) * {{ill|Niinamesai|ja|新嘗祭|12=draft}} (November 26) Remote rituals (遥祀) are conducted for: * {{ill|Saitan-sai|ja|歳旦祭|12=draft}} (January 1) * {{ill|Genshi-sai|ja|元始祭|12=draft}} (January 3) * National Foundation Day Festival (February 11) * {{ill|Kaze-no-Hi-no-Matsuri|ja|風日祈祭|12=draft}} (May 14 and August 4) * {{ill|Tenchō-sai|ja|天長祭|12=draft}} (December 23)Shikinaisha Kenkyukai (1990): p.352 == Access == ; To Gekū (Outer Shrine) * **By Train:** Approximately 5-minute walk from JR {{ill|Sangu Line|ja|参宮線|12=draft}} or Kintetsu {{ill|Yamada Line|ja|近鉄山田線|12=draft}} Ise-shi Station (south exit, JR side) via Gekū Sando.Ise Bunka Publishing (2008): p.33 * **By Bus:** About 5 minutes on foot from Mie Kotsu's "Ise-shi Station" bus stop. * **By Car:** About 5 minutes north from Ise-Nishi IC on the Ise Expressway via Mie Prefectural Route 32. Parking is available nearby with a capacity of 448 cars.Jingu Administration Office "[http://www.isejingu.or.jp/mobile/i/parking/index.html Parking Information]" (accessed September 15, 2013) ; From Gekū to Yamasue Shrine From Gekū, head south along Mikumoto Road (御木本道路) adjacent to the Jingu Forest, turn left at the site of the former Toyomiya-zaki Bunko (豊宮崎文庫), and ascend moss-covered stone steps to reach {{ill|Watarai Okunitama-hime Shrine|ja|度会大国玉比賣神社|12=draft}}, {{ill|Igari Shrine|ja|伊我理神社|12=draft}}, and {{ill|Inaka Shrine|ja|井中神社|12=draft}}.Ise Bunka Publishing (2008): pp.37-38 Continuing approximately 500 meters further along the forest will lead to Yamasue Shrine. == References == {{Reflist}} == 参考文献 == * 宇治山田市役所 編『宇治山田市史 上巻』宇治山田市役所、昭和4th year 1月20日、862p. * [[学研パブリッシング]]『伊勢神宮に行こう』Gakken Mook神社紀行セレクションvol.1、[[薗田稔]]監修、学研マーケティング、2013rd year 7月4日、82p. ISBN 978-4-05-610047-1 * 式内社研究会 編『式内社調査報告 第六巻 東海道1』[[Kogakkan University]]出版部、平成2nd year 2月28日、690p. ISBN 4-87644-080-8 * 『お伊勢さん125社めぐり』別冊『伊勢人』、[[伊勢文化舎]]、平成20th year 12月23日、151p. ISBN 978-4-900759-37-4 == See Also == * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|ja|伊勢国の式内社一覧|12=draft}} * {{ill|List of 125 Shrines of Ise|ja|神宮125社の一覧|lt=List of 125 Shrines of Ise|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|3=伊勢国の式内社一覧|12=draft}} == External Links == * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m02/0216.html 山末神社（やまずえじんじゃ）] - 財団法人伊勢神宮崇敬会 {{神道 横}} {{125 Shrines of Ise}} {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:Branch shrines of Ise Grand Shrine]] [[Category:Shrines in Mie Prefecture]] [[Category:山末神社]] [[Category:伊勢国の式内社]] [[Category:摂末社]] [[Category:伊勢市の神社]] [[Category:refreshing imported pages]] [[Category:bump 1-26]] [[Category:category refresh]] [[Category:125 Shrines of Ise]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:return the see also]] }} [[sn:Yamazue Shrine]] [[ja:山末神社]] [[en:Yamazue Shrine]] [[sn:amazue Shrine]]

{{神社 |名称= 山末神社 | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image= [[File:Yamazue Shrine.jpg|270px]] |所在地= [[三重県]][[伊勢市]][[豊川町 (伊勢市)|豊川町]] |緯度度 = 34 | 緯度分 = 28 | 緯度秒 = 53.1 |経度度 = 136 |経度分 = 42 | 経度秒 = 21.5 |地図= Japan Mie |祭神= 大山津姫命 |社格= [[式内社]]（小）
{{ill|Toyouke Daijingu|ja|豊受大神宮|1=Toyouke Daijingu|2=ja|3=豊受大神宮|4=zh|5=豐受大神宮|12=draft}}摂社 |創建= [[延暦]]23rd year （[[804]]）以前 |本殿= [[神明造]] |別名= |札所等= |例祭= |神事= [[祈年祭]]、[[月次祭]]ほか }} '''Yamazue Shrine''' (山末神社) is a {{ill|branch shrine|ja|摂末社|12=draft}} of {{ill|Toyouke Daijingu|ja|豊受大神宮|1=Toyouke Daijingu|2=ja|3=豊受大神宮|4=zh|5=豐受大神宮|12=draft}} (Outer Shrine) within the {{ill|Ise Grand Shrine|ja|伊勢神宮|12=draft}} complex. It holds the 13th position among the 16 branch shrines of the Outer Shrine.Ujiyamada City Office, ed. (1929), pp. 9, 12. == Overview == Located within the precincts of the Outer Shrine in {{ill|Toyokawa, Ise|ja|豊川町 (伊勢市)|12=draft}}, {{ill|Ise, Mie|ja|伊勢市|12=draft}}, {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県|12=draft}}.Compiled by Ujiyamada City Hall (1929): p.12 It is situated at the southeastern edge of {{ill|Mount Takakura (Ise)|ja|高倉山 (伊勢市)|12=draft}} within the shrine grounds.Gakken Publishing (2013): p.59 However, it is not directly connected to the Outer Shrine’s main approach path, requiring visitors to exit the precinct before entering again. Further south of {{ill|Yamazue Shrine|ja|山末神社|12=draft}} are the {{ill|Tanoe-Ōmizu Shrine|ja|田上大水神社・田上大水御前神社|12=draft}}.Ise Bunka-sha (2008): p.38 The enshrined deity is Ōyama-tsu-hime no Mikoto (大山津姫命), revered as the mountain deity ({{ill|Yama-no-kami|ja|山の神|12=draft}}) of the Outer Shrine’s sacred rice fields and the deity of the spring that irrigates them. These sacred rice fields were once known as "Toyomiyasaki no Mita," sprawling across the area south of Yamasue Shrine, though the land has since been urbanized. In *Ise Province Shrine Name Register Commentary* by Watarai Nobunori, the enshrined deity is referred to as "Ōyamakui no Kami, also known as Yamasue no Ōnushi no Kami." Ōyamakui no Kami (大山咋神) appears in the *{{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記|12=draft}}*, symbolizing the deification of sacred mountains.Compiled by the Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): p.349 The shrine structure is of {{ill|Shinmei-zukuri|ja|神明造|12=draft}} style with a {{ill|ita-buki|ja|板葺|12=draft}} (wooden shingle) roof, surrounded by a {{ill|Tamagaki|ja|玉垣|12=draft}} (sacred fence).Compiled by the Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): p.351 A single {{ill|Shinmei torii|ja|神明鳥居|12=draft}} stands, measuring 8 shaku 3 sun (approximately 2.5 meters) in height. Interestingly, the torii does not face the shrine’s approach but is positioned toward a stone wall. No offering box ({{ill|saisenbako|ja|賽銭箱|12=draft}}) is provided. == History == The name "山末神社" (Yamasue Shrine) refers to its location at the end (末) of Mt. Takakura, where "末" indicates the summit rather than the base of the mountain. In documents such as the "{{ill|Engi Ise Daijingu-shiki|ja|延喜伊勢太神宮式|12=draft}}" and the "{{ill|Toyuke Daijingu Gishikicho|ja|止由気宮儀式帳|12=draft}}", it is written as "山末社". Based on the definition of auxiliary shrines of Ise Grand Shrine, it was established before the compilation of the "{{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|12=draft}}" in 927 (Enchō 5). Additionally, since it is mentioned in the "Toyuke Daijingu Gishikicho," it must have existed prior to 804 (Enryaku 23). Although "{{ill|Yamatohime-no-mikoto|ja|倭姫命世記|1=Yamatohime no Mikoto Seiki|12=draft}}" does not directly mention the founding of Yamasue Shrine, it describes how Yamatohime-no-mikoto viewed the area from Yamasue while searching for a suitable location for the shrine. Rituals ceased by the end of the medieval period, and the shrine's location became unknown during the early modern period. However, through the efforts of the Chief Priest {{ill|Kawabe Kiyoshige|ja|河辺精長|12=draft}}, the shrine was restored at its current site on June 23, 1663 (Kanbun 3, Julian calendar: July 27, 1663).Shikinaisha Kenkyukai (1990): p.350 During its restoration, references from "{{ill|Shinmei Hisho|ja|神名秘書|12=draft}}" indicating "Yamasue Shrine, located in Miyama, Ohnashi Valley, south of Mitaguchi Shrine, Tsugihashi-go" were consulted for field surveys. The shrine was reconstructed on January 1, 1921 (Taisho 10), and again in July 1957 (Showa 32), with major renovations carried out on October 27, 1983 (Showa 58). == Rituals == The following rituals are performed by a group of three priests: Gon-negi, Miyakujou, and Shutsushi, in a巡回祭典 (巡回 ritual) format: * {{ill|Kinen-sai|ja|祈年祭|12=draft}} (February 20) * {{ill|Tsukinami-sai|ja|月次祭|1=Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|12=draft}} (June 20 and December 20) * {{ill|Kannamesai|ja|神嘗祭|12=draft}} (October 20) * {{ill|Niinamesai|ja|新嘗祭|12=draft}} (November 26) Remote rituals (遥祀) are conducted for: * {{ill|Saitan-sai|ja|歳旦祭|12=draft}} (January 1) * {{ill|Genshi-sai|ja|元始祭|12=draft}} (January 3) * National Foundation Day Festival (February 11) * {{ill|Kaze-no-Hi-no-Matsuri|ja|風日祈祭|12=draft}} (May 14 and August 4) * {{ill|Tenchō-sai|ja|天長祭|12=draft}} (December 23)Shikinaisha Kenkyukai (1990): p.352 == Access == ; To Gekū (Outer Shrine) * **By Train:** Approximately 5-minute walk from JR {{ill|Sangu Line|ja|参宮線|12=draft}} or Kintetsu {{ill|Yamada Line|ja|近鉄山田線|12=draft}} Ise-shi Station (south exit, JR side) via Gekū Sando.Ise Bunka Publishing (2008): p.33 * **By Bus:** About 5 minutes on foot from Mie Kotsu's "Ise-shi Station" bus stop. * **By Car:** About 5 minutes north from Ise-Nishi IC on the Ise Expressway via Mie Prefectural Route 32. Parking is available nearby with a capacity of 448 cars.Jingu Administration Office "[http://www.isejingu.or.jp/mobile/i/parking/index.html Parking Information]" (accessed September 15, 2013) ; From Gekū to Yamasue Shrine From Gekū, head south along Mikumoto Road (御木本道路) adjacent to the Jingu Forest, turn left at the site of the former Toyomiya-zaki Bunko (豊宮崎文庫), and ascend moss-covered stone steps to reach {{ill|Watarai Okunitama-hime Shrine|ja|度会大国玉比賣神社|12=draft}}, {{ill|Igari Shrine|ja|伊我理神社|12=draft}}, and {{ill|Inaka Shrine|ja|井中神社|12=draft}}.Ise Bunka Publishing (2008): pp.37-38 Continuing approximately 500 meters further along the forest will lead to Yamasue Shrine. == References == {{Reflist}} == 参考文献 == * 宇治山田市役所 編『宇治山田市史 上巻』宇治山田市役所、昭和4th year 1月20日、862p. * [[学研パブリッシング]]『伊勢神宮に行こう』Gakken Mook神社紀行セレクションvol.1、[[薗田稔]]監修、学研マーケティング、2013rd year 7月4日、82p. ISBN 978-4-05-610047-1 * 式内社研究会 編『式内社調査報告 第六巻 東海道1』[[Kogakkan University]]出版部、平成2nd year 2月28日、690p. ISBN 4-87644-080-8 * 『お伊勢さん125社めぐり』別冊『伊勢人』、[[伊勢文化舎]]、平成20th year 12月23日、151p. ISBN 978-4-900759-37-4 == See Also == * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|ja|伊勢国の式内社一覧|12=draft}} * {{ill|List of 125 Shrines of Ise|ja|神宮125社の一覧|lt=List of 125 Shrines of Ise|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|3=伊勢国の式内社一覧|12=draft}} == External Links == * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m02/0216.html 山末神社（やまずえじんじゃ）] - 財団法人伊勢神宮崇敬会 {{神道 横}} {{125 Shrines of Ise}} {{draft categories|}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Branch shrines of Ise Grand Shrine]] [[Category:Shrines in Mie Prefecture]] [[Category:山末神社]] [[Category:伊勢国の式内社]] [[Category:摂末社]] [[Category:伊勢市の神社]] [[Category:refreshing imported pages]] [[Category:bump 1-26]] [[Category:category refresh]] [[Category:125 Shrines of Ise]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:return the see also]] }} [[sn:Yamazue Shrine]] [[ja:山末神社]] [[en:Yamazue Shrine]] [[sn:amazue Shrine]]

No offering box ({{ill|saisenbako|ja|賽銭箱|12=draft}}) is provided. == History == The name "山末神社" (Yamasue Shrine) refers to its location at the end (末) of Mt. Takakura, where "末" indicates the summit rather than the base of the mountain. In documents such as the "{{ill|Engi Ise Daijingu-shiki|ja|延喜伊勢太神宮式|12=draft}}" and the "{{ill|Toyuke Daijingu Gishikicho|ja|止由気宮儀式帳|12=draft}}", it is written as "山末社". Based on the definition of auxiliary shrines of Ise Grand Shrine, it was established before the compilation of the "{{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|12=draft}}" in 927 (Enchō 5). Additionally, since it is mentioned in the "Toyuke Daijingu Gishikicho," it must have existed prior to 804 (Enryaku 23). Although "{{ill|Yamatohime-no-mikoto|ja|倭姫命世記|1=Yamatohime no Mikoto Seiki|12=draft}}" does not directly mention the founding of Yamasue Shrine, it describes how Yamatohime-no-mikoto viewed the area from Yamasue while searching for a suitable location for the shrine. Rituals ceased by the end of the medieval period, and the shrine's location became unknown during the early modern period. However, through the efforts of the Chief Priest {{ill|Kawabe Kiyoshige|ja|河辺精長|12=draft}}, the shrine was restored at its current site on June 23, 1663 (Kanbun 3, Julian calendar: July 27, 1663).Shikinaisha Kenkyukai (1990): p.350 During its restoration, references from "{{ill|Shinmei Hisho|ja|神名秘書|12=draft}}" indicating "Yamasue Shrine, located in Miyama, Ohnashi Valley, south of Mitaguchi Shrine, Tsugihashi-go" were consulted for field surveys. The shrine was reconstructed on January 1, 1921 (Taisho 10), and again in July 1957 (Showa 32), with major renovations carried out on October 27, 1983 (Showa 58). == Rituals == The following rituals are performed by a group of three priests: Gon-negi, Miyakujou, and Shutsushi, in a巡回祭典 (巡回 ritual) format: * {{ill|Kinen-sai|ja|祈年祭|12=draft}} (February 20) * {{ill|Tsukinami-sai|ja|月次祭|1=Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|12=draft}} (June 20 and December 20) * {{ill|Kannamesai|ja|神嘗祭|12=draft}} (October 20) * {{ill|Niinamesai|ja|新嘗祭|12=draft}} (November 26) Remote rituals (遥祀) are conducted for: * {{ill|Saitan-sai|ja|歳旦祭|12=draft}} (January 1) * {{ill|Genshi-sai|ja|元始祭|12=draft}} (January 3) * National Foundation Day Festival (February 11) * {{ill|Kaze-no-Hi-no-Matsuri|ja|風日祈祭|12=draft}} (May 14 and August 4) * {{ill|Tenchō-sai|ja|天長祭|12=draft}} (December 23)Shikinaisha Kenkyukai (1990): p.352 == Access == ; To Gekū (Outer Shrine) * **By Train:** Approximately 5-minute walk from JR {{ill|Sangu Line|ja|参宮線|12=draft}} or Kintetsu {{ill|Yamada Line|ja|近鉄山田線|12=draft}} Ise-shi Station (south exit, JR side) via Gekū Sando.Ise Bunka Publishing (2008): p.33 * **By Bus:** About 5 minutes on foot from Mie Kotsu's "Ise-shi Station" bus stop. * **By Car:** About 5 minutes north from Ise-Nishi IC on the Ise Expressway via Mie Prefectural Route 32. Parking is available nearby with a capacity of 448 cars.Jingu Administration Office "[http://www.isejingu.or.jp/mobile/i/parking/index.html Parking Information]" (accessed September 15, 2013) ; From Gekū to Yamasue Shrine From Gekū, head south along Mikumoto Road (御木本道路) adjacent to the Jingu Forest, turn left at the site of the former Toyomiya-zaki Bunko (豊宮崎文庫), and ascend moss-covered stone steps to reach {{ill|Watarai Okunitama-hime Shrine|ja|度会大国玉比賣神社|12=draft}}, {{ill|Igari Shrine|ja|伊我理神社|12=draft}}, and {{ill|Inaka Shrine|ja|井中神社|12=draft}}.Ise Bunka Publishing (2008): pp.37-38 Continuing approximately 500 meters further along the forest will lead to Yamasue Shrine. == References == {{Reflist}} == 参考文献 == * 宇治山田市役所 編『宇治山田市史 上巻』宇治山田市役所、昭和4th year 1月20日、862p. * [[学研パブリッシング]]『伊勢神宮に行こう』Gakken Mook神社紀行セレクションvol.1、[[薗田稔]]監修、学研マーケティング、2013rd year 7月4日、82p. ISBN 978-4-05-610047-1 * 式内社研究会 編『式内社調査報告 第六巻 東海道1』[[Kogakkan University]]出版部、平成2nd year 2月28日、690p. ISBN 4-87644-080-8 * 『お伊勢さん125社めぐり』別冊『伊勢人』、[[伊勢文化舎]]、平成20th year 12月23日、151p. ISBN 978-4-900759-37-4 == See Also == * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|ja|伊勢国の式内社一覧|12=draft}} * {{ill|List of 125 Shrines of Ise|ja|神宮125社の一覧|lt=List of 125 Shrines of Ise|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|3=伊勢国の式内社一覧|12=draft}} == External Links == * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m02/0216.html 山末神社（やまずえじんじゃ）] - 財団法人伊勢神宮崇敬会 {{神道 横}} {{125 Shrines of Ise}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Branch shrines of Ise Grand Shrine]] [[Category:Shrines in Mie Prefecture]] [[Category:山末神社]] [[Category:伊勢国の式内社]] [[Category:摂末社]] [[Category:伊勢市の神社]] [[Category:refreshing imported pages]] [[Category:bump 1-26]] [[Category:category refresh]] [[Category:125 Shrines of Ise]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:return the see also]] }} [[sn:Yamazue Shrine]] [[ja:山末神社]] [[en:Yamazue Shrine]] [[sn:amazue Shrine]]

{{神社 |名称= 山末神社 | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image= [[File:Yamazue Shrine.jpg|270px]] |所在地= [[三重県]][[伊勢市]][[豊川町 (伊勢市)|豊川町]] |緯度度 = 34 | 緯度分 = 28 | 緯度秒 = 53.1 |経度度 = 136 |経度分 = 42 | 経度秒 = 21.5 |地図= Japan Mie |祭神= 大山津姫命 |社格= [[式内社]]（小）
{{ill|Toyouke Daijingu|ja|豊受大神宮|1=Toyouke Daijingu|2=ja|3=豊受大神宮|4=zh|5=豐受大神宮|12=draft}}摂社 |創建= [[延暦]]23rd year （[[804]]）以前 |本殿= [[神明造]] |別名= |札所等= |例祭= |神事= [[祈年祭]]、[[月次祭]]ほか }} '''Yamazue Shrine''' (山末神社) is a {{ill|branch shrine|ja|摂末社|12=draft}} of {{ill|Toyouke Daijingu|ja|豊受大神宮|1=Toyouke Daijingu|2=ja|3=豊受大神宮|4=zh|5=豐受大神宮|12=draft}} (Outer Shrine) within the {{ill|Ise Grand Shrine|ja|伊勢神宮|12=draft}} complex. It holds the 13th position among the 16 branch shrines of the Outer Shrine.Ujiyamada City Office, ed. (1929), pp. 9, 12. == Overview == Located within the precincts of the Outer Shrine in {{ill|Toyokawa, Ise|ja|豊川町 (伊勢市)|12=draft}}, {{ill|Ise, Mie|ja|伊勢市|12=draft}}, {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県|12=draft}}.Compiled by Ujiyamada City Hall (1929): p.12 It is situated at the southeastern edge of {{ill|Mount Takakura (Ise)|ja|高倉山 (伊勢市)|12=draft}} within the shrine grounds.Gakken Publishing (2013): p.59 However, it is not directly connected to the Outer Shrine’s main approach path, requiring visitors to exit the precinct before entering again. Further south of {{ill|Yamazue Shrine|ja|山末神社|12=draft}} are the {{ill|Tanoe-Ōmizu Shrine|ja|田上大水神社・田上大水御前神社|12=draft}}.Ise Bunka-sha (2008): p.38 The enshrined deity is Ōyama-tsu-hime no Mikoto (大山津姫命), revered as the mountain deity ({{ill|Yama-no-kami|ja|山の神|12=draft}}) of the Outer Shrine’s sacred rice fields and the deity of the spring that irrigates them. Interestingly, the torii does not face the shrine’s approach but is positioned toward a stone wall. ISBN 978-4-05-610047-1 * 式内社研究会 編『式内社調査報告 第六巻 東海道1』[[Kogakkan University]]出版部、平成2nd year 2月28日、690p. ISBN 4-87644-080-8 * 『お伊勢さん125社めぐり』別冊『伊勢人』、[[伊勢文化舎]]、平成20th year 12月23日、151p. ISBN 978-4-900759-37-4 == See Also == * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|ja|伊勢国の式内社一覧|12=draft}} * {{ill|List of 125 Shrines of Ise|ja|神宮125社の一覧|lt=List of 125 Shrines of Ise|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|3=伊勢国の式内社一覧|12=draft}} == External Links == * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m02/0216.html 山末神社（やまずえじんじゃ）] - 財団法人伊勢神宮崇敬会 {{神道 横}} {{125 Shrines of Ise}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Branch shrines of Ise Grand Shrine]] [[Category:Shrines in Mie Prefecture]] [[Category:山末神社]] [[Category:伊勢国の式内社]] [[Category:摂末社]] [[Category:伊勢市の神社]] [[Category:refreshing imported pages]] [[Category:category refresh]] [[Category:125 Shrines of Ise]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:return the see also]] }} [[sn:Yamazue Shrine]] [[ja:山末神社]] [[en:Yamazue Shrine]] [[sn:amazue Shrine]]

revision.text:
{{神社 |名称= 山末神社 | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image= [[File:Yamazue Shrine.jpg|270px]] |所在地= [[三重県]][[伊勢市]][[豊川町 (伊勢市)|豊川町]] |緯度度 = 34 | 緯度分 = 28 | 緯度秒 = 53.1 |経度度 = 136 |経度分 = 42 | 経度秒 = 21.5 |地図= Japan Mie |祭神= 大山津姫命 |社格= [[式内社]]（小）
{{ill|Toyouke Daijingu|ja|豊受大神宮|1=Toyouke Daijingu|2=ja|3=豊受大神宮|4=zh|5=豐受大神宮|12=draft}}摂社 |創建= [[延暦]]23rd year （[[804]]）以前 |本殿= [[神明造]] |別名= |札所等= |例祭= |神事= [[祈年祭]]、[[月次祭]]ほか }} '''Yamazue Shrine''' (山末神社) is a {{ill|branch shrine|ja|摂末社|12=draft}} of {{ill|Toyouke Daijingu|ja|豊受大神宮|1=Toyouke Daijingu|2=ja|3=豊受大神宮|4=zh|5=豐受大神宮|12=draft}} (Outer Shrine) within the {{ill|Ise Grand Shrine|ja|伊勢神宮|12=draft}} complex. It holds the 13th position among the 16 branch shrines of the Outer Shrine.Ujiyamada City Office, ed. (1929), pp. 9, 12. == Overview == Located within the precincts of the Outer Shrine in {{ill|Toyokawa, Ise|ja|豊川町 (伊勢市)|12=draft}}, {{ill|Ise, Mie|ja|伊勢市|12=draft}}, {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県|12=draft}}.Compiled by Ujiyamada City Hall (1929): p.12 It is situated at the southeastern edge of {{ill|Mount Takakura (Ise)|ja|高倉山 (伊勢市)|12=draft}} within the shrine grounds.Gakken Publishing (2013): p.59 However, it is not directly connected to the Outer Shrine’s main approach path, requiring visitors to exit the precinct before entering again. Further south of {{ill|Yamazue Shrine|ja|山末神社|12=draft}} are the {{ill|Tanoe-Ōmizu Shrine|ja|田上大水神社・田上大水御前神社|12=draft}}.Ise Bunka-sha (2008): p.38 The enshrined deity is Ōyama-tsu-hime no Mikoto (大山津姫命), revered as the mountain deity ({{ill|Yama-no-kami|ja|山の神|12=draft}}) of the Outer Shrine’s sacred rice fields and the deity of the spring that irrigates them. These sacred rice fields were once known as "Toyomiyasaki no Mita," sprawling across the area south of Yamasue Shrine, though the land has since been urbanized. In *Ise Province Shrine Name Register Commentary* by Watarai Nobunori, the enshrined deity is referred to as "Ōyamakui no Kami, also known as Yamasue no Ōnushi no Kami." Ōyamakui no Kami (大山咋神) appears in the *{{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記|12=draft}}*, symbolizing the deification of sacred mountains.Compiled by the Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): p.349 The shrine structure is of {{ill|Shinmei-zukuri|ja|神明造|12=draft}} style with a {{ill|ita-buki|ja|板葺|12=draft}} (wooden shingle) roof, surrounded by a {{ill|Tamagaki|ja|玉垣|12=draft}} (sacred fence).Compiled by the Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): p.351 A single {{ill|Shinmei torii|ja|神明鳥居|12=draft}} stands, measuring 8 shaku 3 sun (approximately 2.5 meters) in height. Interestingly, the torii does not face the shrine’s approach but is positioned toward a stone wall. No offering box ({{ill|saisenbako|ja|賽銭箱|12=draft}}) is provided. == History == The name "山末神社" (Yamasue Shrine) refers to its location at the end (末) of Mt. Takakura, where "末" indicates the summit rather than the base of the mountain. In documents such as the "{{ill|Engi Ise Daijingu-shiki|ja|延喜伊勢太神宮式|12=draft}}" and the "{{ill|Toyuke Daijingu Gishikicho|ja|止由気宮儀式帳|12=draft}}", it is written as "山末社". Based on the definition of auxiliary shrines of Ise Grand Shrine, it was established before the compilation of the "{{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|12=draft}}" in 927 (Enchō 5). Additionally, since it is mentioned in the "Toyuke Daijingu Gishikicho," it must have existed prior to 804 (Enryaku 23). Although "{{ill|Yamatohime-no-mikoto|ja|倭姫命世記|1=Yamatohime no Mikoto Seiki|12=draft}}" does not directly mention the founding of Yamasue Shrine, it describes how Yamatohime-no-mikoto viewed the area from Yamasue while searching for a suitable location for the shrine. Rituals ceased by the end of the medieval period, and the shrine's location became unknown during the early modern period. However, through the efforts of the Chief Priest {{ill|Kawabe Kiyoshige|ja|河辺精長|12=draft}}, the shrine was restored at its current site on June 23, 1663 (Kanbun 3, Julian calendar: July 27, 1663).Shikinaisha Kenkyukai (1990): p.350 During its restoration, references from "{{ill|Shinmei Hisho|ja|神名秘書|12=draft}}" indicating "Yamasue Shrine, located in Miyama, Ohnashi Valley, south of Mitaguchi Shrine, Tsugihashi-go" were consulted for field surveys. The shrine was reconstructed on January 1, 1921 (Taisho 10), and again in July 1957 (Showa 32), with major renovations carried out on October 27, 1983 (Showa 58). == Rituals == The following rituals are performed by a group of three priests: Gon-negi, Miyakujou, and Shutsushi, in a巡回祭典 (巡回 ritual) format: * {{ill|Kinen-sai|ja|祈年祭|12=draft}} (February 20) * {{ill|Tsukinami-sai|ja|月次祭|1=Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|12=draft}} (June 20 and December 20) * {{ill|Kannamesai|ja|神嘗祭|12=draft}} (October 20) * {{ill|Niinamesai|ja|新嘗祭|12=draft}} (November 26) Remote rituals (遥祀) are conducted for: * {{ill|Saitan-sai|ja|歳旦祭|12=draft}} (January 1) * {{ill|Genshi-sai|ja|元始祭|12=draft}} (January 3) * National Foundation Day Festival (February 11) * {{ill|Kaze-no-Hi-no-Matsuri|ja|風日祈祭|12=draft}} (May 14 and August 4) * {{ill|Tenchō-sai|ja|天長祭|12=draft}} (December 23)Shikinaisha Kenkyukai (1990): p.352 == Access == ; To Gekū (Outer Shrine) * **By Train:** Approximately 5-minute walk from JR {{ill|Sangu Line|ja|参宮線|12=draft}} or Kintetsu {{ill|Yamada Line|ja|近鉄山田線|12=draft}} Ise-shi Station (south exit, JR side) via Gekū Sando.Ise Bunka Publishing (2008): p.33 * **By Bus:** About 5 minutes on foot from Mie Kotsu's "Ise-shi Station" bus stop. * **By Car:** About 5 minutes north from Ise-Nishi IC on the Ise Expressway via Mie Prefectural Route 32. Parking is available nearby with a capacity of 448 cars.Jingu Administration Office "[http://www.isejingu.or.jp/mobile/i/parking/index.html Parking Information]" (accessed September 15, 2013) ; From Gekū to Yamasue Shrine From Gekū, head south along Mikumoto Road (御木本道路) adjacent to the Jingu Forest, turn left at the site of the former Toyomiya-zaki Bunko (豊宮崎文庫), and ascend moss-covered stone steps to reach {{ill|Watarai Okunitama-hime Shrine|ja|度会大国玉比賣神社|12=draft}}, {{ill|Igari Shrine|ja|伊我理神社|12=draft}}, and {{ill|Inaka Shrine|ja|井中神社|12=draft}}.Ise Bunka Publishing (2008): pp.37-38 Continuing approximately 500 meters further along the forest will lead to Yamasue Shrine. == References == {{Reflist}} == 参考文献 == * 宇治山田市役所 編『宇治山田市史 上巻』宇治山田市役所、昭和4th year 1月20日、862p. * [[学研パブリッシング]]『伊勢神宮に行こう』Gakken Mook神社紀行セレクションvol.1、[[薗田稔]]監修、学研マーケティング、2013rd year 7月4日、82p. ISBN 978-4-05-610047-1 * 式内社研究会 編『式内社調査報告 第六巻 東海道1』[[Kogakkan University]]出版部、平成2nd year 2月28日、690p. ISBN 4-87644-080-8 * 『お伊勢さん125社めぐり』別冊『伊勢人』、[[伊勢文化舎]]、平成20th year 12月23日、151p. ISBN 978-4-900759-37-4 == See Also == * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|ja|伊勢国の式内社一覧|12=draft}} * {{ill|List of 125 Shrines of Ise|ja|神宮125社の一覧|lt=List of 125 Shrines of Ise|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|3=伊勢国の式内社一覧|12=draft}} == External Links == * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m02/0216.html 山末神社（やまずえじんじゃ）] - 財団法人伊勢神宮崇敬会 {{神道 横}} {{125 Shrines of Ise}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Branch shrines of Ise Grand Shrine]] [[Category:Shrines in Mie Prefecture]] [[Category:山末神社]] [[Category:伊勢国の式内社]] [[Category:摂末社]] [[Category:伊勢市の神社]] [[Category:refreshing imported pages]] [[Category:category refresh]] [[Category:125 Shrines of Ise]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]] }} [[sn:Yamazue Shrine]] [[ja:山末神社]] [[en:Yamazue Shrine]] [[sn:amazue Shrine]]

revision.text:
{{神社 |名称= 山末神社 | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image= [[File:Yamazue Shrine.jpg|270px]] |所在地= [[三重県]][[伊勢市]][[豊川町 (伊勢市)|豊川町]] |緯度度 = 34 | 緯度分 = 28 | 緯度秒 = 53.1 |経度度 = 136 |経度分 = 42 | 経度秒 = 21.5 |地図= Japan Mie |祭神= 大山津姫命 |社格= [[式内社]]（小）
{{ill|Toyouke Daijingu|ja|豊受大神宮|1=Toyouke Daijingu|2=ja|3=豊受大神宮|4=zh|5=豐受大神宮|12=draft|12=draft}}摂社 |創建= [[延暦]]23rd year （[[804]]）以前 |本殿= [[神明造]] |別名= |札所等= |例祭= |神事= [[祈年祭]]、[[月次祭]]ほか }} '''Yamazue Shrine''' (山末神社) is a {{ill|branch shrine|ja|摂末社|12=draft|12=draft}} of {{ill|Toyouke Daijingu|ja|豊受大神宮|1=Toyouke Daijingu|2=ja|3=豊受大神宮|4=zh|5=豐受大神宮|12=draft|12=draft}} (Outer Shrine) within the {{ill|Ise Grand Shrine|ja|伊勢神宮|12=draft|12=draft}} complex. It holds the 13th position among the 16 branch shrines of the Outer Shrine.Ujiyamada City Office, ed. (1929), pp. 9, 12. == Overview == Located within the precincts of the Outer Shrine in {{ill|Toyokawa, Ise|ja|豊川町 (伊勢市)|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Ise, Mie|ja|伊勢市|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県|12=draft|12=draft}}.Compiled by Ujiyamada City Hall (1929): p.12 It is situated at the southeastern edge of {{ill|Mount Takakura (Ise)|ja|高倉山 (伊勢市)|12=draft|12=draft}} within the shrine grounds.Gakken Publishing (2013): p.59 However, it is not directly connected to the Outer Shrine’s main approach path, requiring visitors to exit the precinct before entering again. Further south of {{ill|Yamazue Shrine|ja|山末神社|12=draft|12=draft}} are the {{ill|Tanoe-Ōmizu Shrine|ja|田上大水神社・田上大水御前神社|12=draft|12=draft}}.Ise Bunka-sha (2008): p.38 The enshrined deity is Ōyama-tsu-hime no Mikoto (大山津姫命), revered as the mountain deity ({{ill|Yama-no-kami|ja|山の神|12=draft|12=draft}}) of the Outer Shrine’s sacred rice fields and the deity of the spring that irrigates them. These sacred rice fields were once known as "Toyomiyasaki no Mita," sprawling across the area south of Yamasue Shrine, though the land has since been urbanized. In *Ise Province Shrine Name Register Commentary* by Watarai Nobunori, the enshrined deity is referred to as "Ōyamakui no Kami, also known as Yamasue no Ōnushi no Kami." Ōyamakui no Kami (大山咋神) appears in the *{{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記|12=draft|12=draft}}*, symbolizing the deification of sacred mountains.Compiled by the Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): p.349 The shrine structure is of {{ill|Shinmei-zukuri|ja|神明造|12=draft|12=draft}} style with a {{ill|ita-buki|ja|板葺|12=draft|12=draft}} (wooden shingle) roof, surrounded by a {{ill|Tamagaki|ja|玉垣|12=draft|12=draft}} (sacred fence).Compiled by the Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): p.351 A single {{ill|Shinmei torii|ja|神明鳥居|12=draft|12=draft}} stands, measuring 8 shaku 3 sun (approximately 2.5 meters) in height. Interestingly, the torii does not face the shrine’s approach but is positioned toward a stone wall. No offering box ({{ill|saisenbako|ja|賽銭箱|12=draft|12=draft}}) is provided. == History == The name "山末神社" (Yamasue Shrine) refers to its location at the end (末) of Mt. Takakura, where "末" indicates the summit rather than the base of the mountain. In documents such as the "{{ill|Engi Ise Daijingu-shiki|ja|延喜伊勢太神宮式|12=draft|12=draft}}" and the "{{ill|Toyuke Daijingu Gishikicho|ja|止由気宮儀式帳|12=draft|12=draft}}", it is written as "山末社". Based on the definition of auxiliary shrines of Ise Grand Shrine, it was established before the compilation of the "{{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|12=draft|12=draft}}" in 927 (Enchō 5). Additionally, since it is mentioned in the "Toyuke Daijingu Gishikicho," it must have existed prior to 804 (Enryaku 23). Although "{{ill|Yamatohime-no-mikoto|ja|倭姫命世記|1=Yamatohime no Mikoto Seiki|12=draft|12=draft}}" does not directly mention the founding of Yamasue Shrine, it describes how Yamatohime-no-mikoto viewed the area from Yamasue while searching for a suitable location for the shrine. Rituals ceased by the end of the medieval period, and the shrine's location became unknown during the early modern period. However, through the efforts of the Chief Priest {{ill|Kawabe Kiyoshige|ja|河辺精長|12=draft|12=draft}}, the shrine was restored at its current site on June 23, 1663 (Kanbun 3, Julian calendar: July 27, 1663).Shikinaisha Kenkyukai (1990): p.350 During its restoration, references from "{{ill|Shinmei Hisho|ja|神名秘書|12=draft|12=draft}}" indicating "Yamasue Shrine, located in Miyama, Ohnashi Valley, south of Mitaguchi Shrine, Tsugihashi-go" were consulted for field surveys. The shrine was reconstructed on January 1, 1921 (Taisho 10), and again in July 1957 (Showa 32), with major renovations carried out on October 27, 1983 (Showa 58). == Rituals == The following rituals are performed by a group of three priests: Gon-negi, Miyakujou, and Shutsushi, in a巡回祭典 (巡回 ritual) format: * {{ill|Kinen-sai|ja|祈年祭|12=draft|12=draft}} (February 20) * {{ill|Tsukinami-sai|ja|月次祭|1=Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|12=draft|12=draft}} (June 20 and December 20) * {{ill|Kannamesai|ja|神嘗祭|12=draft|12=draft}} (October 20) * {{ill|Niinamesai|ja|新嘗祭|12=draft|12=draft}} (November 26) Remote rituals (遥祀) are conducted for: * {{ill|Saitan-sai|ja|歳旦祭|12=draft|12=draft}} (January 1) * {{ill|Genshi-sai|ja|元始祭|12=draft|12=draft}} (January 3) * National Foundation Day Festival (February 11) * {{ill|Kaze-no-Hi-no-Matsuri|ja|風日祈祭|12=draft|12=draft}} (May 14 and August 4) * {{ill|Tenchō-sai|ja|天長祭|12=draft|12=draft}} (December 23)Shikinaisha Kenkyukai (1990): p.352 == Access == ; To Gekū (Outer Shrine) * **By Train:** Approximately 5-minute walk from JR {{ill|Sangu Line|ja|参宮線|12=draft|12=draft}} or Kintetsu {{ill|Yamada Line|ja|近鉄山田線|12=draft|12=draft}} Ise-shi Station (south exit, JR side) via Gekū Sando.Ise Bunka Publishing (2008): p.33 * **By Bus:** About 5 minutes on foot from Mie Kotsu's "Ise-shi Station" bus stop. * **By Car:** About 5 minutes north from Ise-Nishi IC on the Ise Expressway via Mie Prefectural Route 32. Parking is available nearby with a capacity of 448 cars.Jingu Administration Office "[http://www.isejingu.or.jp/mobile/i/parking/index.html Parking Information]" (accessed September 15, 2013) ; From Gekū to Yamasue Shrine From Gekū, head south along Mikumoto Road (御木本道路) adjacent to the Jingu Forest, turn left at the site of the former Toyomiya-zaki Bunko (豊宮崎文庫), and ascend moss-covered stone steps to reach {{ill|Watarai Okunitama-hime Shrine|ja|度会大国玉比賣神社|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Igari Shrine|ja|伊我理神社|12=draft|12=draft}}, and {{ill|Inaka Shrine|ja|井中神社|12=draft|12=draft}}.Ise Bunka Publishing (2008): pp.37-38 Continuing approximately 500 meters further along the forest will lead to Yamasue Shrine. == References == {{Reflist}} == 参考文献 == * 宇治山田市役所 編『宇治山田市史 上巻』宇治山田市役所、昭和4th year 1月20日、862p. * [[学研パブリッシング]]『伊勢神宮に行こう』Gakken Mook神社紀行セレクションvol.1、[[薗田稔]]監修、学研マーケティング、2013rd year 7月4日、82p. ISBN 978-4-05-610047-1 * 式内社研究会 編『式内社調査報告 第六巻 東海道1』[[Kogakkan University]]出版部、平成2nd year 2月28日、690p. ISBN 4-87644-080-8 * 『お伊勢さん125社めぐり』別冊『伊勢人』、[[伊勢文化舎]]、平成20th year 12月23日、151p. ISBN 978-4-900759-37-4 == See Also == * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|ja|伊勢国の式内社一覧|12=draft|12=draft}} * {{ill|List of 125 Shrines of Ise|ja|神宮125社の一覧|lt=List of 125 Shrines of Ise|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|3=伊勢国の式内社一覧|12=draft|12=draft}} == External Links == * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m02/0216.html 山末神社（やまずえじんじゃ）] - 財団法人伊勢神宮崇敬会 {{神道 横}} {{125 Shrines of Ise}} {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:Branch shrines of Ise Grand Shrine]] [[Category:Shrines in Mie Prefecture]] [[Category:山末神社]] [[Category:伊勢国の式内社]] [[Category:摂末社]] [[Category:伊勢市の神社]] [[Category:category refresh]] [[Category:125 Shrines of Ise]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]] }} [[sn:Yamazue Shrine]] [[ja:山末神社]] [[en:Yamazue Shrine]] [[sn:amazue Shrine]]

Takakura, where "末" indicates the summit rather than the base of the mountain. In documents such as the "{{ill|Engi Ise Daijingu-shiki|ja|延喜伊勢太神宮式|12=draft|12=draft}}" and the "{{ill|Toyuke Daijingu Gishikicho|ja|止由気宮儀式帳|12=draft|12=draft}}", it is written as "山末社". Based on the definition of auxiliary shrines of Ise Grand Shrine, it was established before the compilation of the "{{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|12=draft|12=draft}}" in 927 (Enchō 5). Additionally, since it is mentioned in the "Toyuke Daijingu Gishikicho," it must have existed prior to 804 (Enryaku 23). Although "{{ill|Yamatohime-no-mikoto|ja|倭姫命世記|1=Yamatohime no Mikoto Seiki|12=draft|12=draft}}" does not directly mention the founding of Yamasue Shrine, it describes how Yamatohime-no-mikoto viewed the area from Yamasue while searching for a suitable location for the shrine. Rituals ceased by the end of the medieval period, and the shrine's location became unknown during the early modern period. However, through the efforts of the Chief Priest {{ill|Kawabe Kiyoshige|ja|河辺精長|12=draft|12=draft}}, the shrine was restored at its current site on June 23, 1663 (Kanbun 3, Julian calendar: July 27, 1663).Shikinaisha Kenkyukai (1990): p.350 During its restoration, references from "{{ill|Shinmei Hisho|ja|神名秘書|12=draft|12=draft}}" indicating "Yamasue Shrine, located in Miyama, Ohnashi Valley, south of Mitaguchi Shrine, Tsugihashi-go" were consulted for field surveys. The shrine was reconstructed on January 1, 1921 (Taisho 10), and again in July 1957 (Showa 32), with major renovations carried out on October 27, 1983 (Showa 58). == Rituals == The following rituals are performed by a group of three priests: Gon-negi, Miyakujou, and Shutsushi, in a巡回祭典 (巡回 ritual) format: * {{ill|Kinen-sai|ja|祈年祭|12=draft|12=draft}} (February 20) * {{ill|Tsukinami-sai|ja|月次祭|1=Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|12=draft|12=draft}} (June 20 and December 20) * {{ill|Kannamesai|ja|神嘗祭|12=draft|12=draft}} (October 20) * {{ill|Niinamesai|ja|新嘗祭|12=draft|12=draft}} (November 26) Remote rituals (遥祀) are conducted for: * {{ill|Saitan-sai|ja|歳旦祭|12=draft|12=draft}} (January 1) * {{ill|Genshi-sai|ja|元始祭|12=draft|12=draft}} (January 3) * National Foundation Day Festival (February 11) * {{ill|Kaze-no-Hi-no-Matsuri|ja|風日祈祭|12=draft|12=draft}} (May 14 and August 4) * {{ill|Tenchō-sai|ja|天長祭|12=draft|12=draft}} (December 23)Shikinaisha Kenkyukai (1990): p.352 == Access == ; To Gekū (Outer Shrine) * **By Train:** Approximately 5-minute walk from JR {{ill|Sangu Line|ja|参宮線|12=draft|12=draft}} or Kintetsu {{ill|Yamada Line|ja|近鉄山田線|12=draft|12=draft}} Ise-shi Station (south exit, JR side) via Gekū Sando.Ise Bunka Publishing (2008): p.33 * **By Bus:** About 5 minutes on foot from Mie Kotsu's "Ise-shi Station" bus stop. * **By Car:** About 5 minutes north from Ise-Nishi IC on the Ise Expressway via Mie Prefectural Route 32. Parking is available nearby with a capacity of 448 cars.Jingu Administration Office "[http://www.isejingu.or.jp/mobile/i/parking/index.html Parking Information]" (accessed September 15, 2013) ; From Gekū to Yamasue Shrine From Gekū, head south along Mikumoto Road (御木本道路) adjacent to the Jingu Forest, turn left at the site of the former Toyomiya-zaki Bunko (豊宮崎文庫), and ascend moss-covered stone steps to reach {{ill|Watarai Okunitama-hime Shrine|ja|度会大国玉比賣神社|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Igari Shrine|ja|伊我理神社|12=draft|12=draft}}, and {{ill|Inaka Shrine|ja|井中神社|12=draft|12=draft}}.Ise Bunka Publishing (2008): pp.37-38 Continuing approximately 500 meters further along the forest will lead to Yamasue Shrine. == References == {{Reflist}} == 参考文献 == * 宇治山田市役所 編『宇治山田市史 上巻』宇治山田市役所、昭和4th year 1月20日、862p. * [[学研パブリッシング]]『伊勢神宮に行こう』Gakken Mook神社紀行セレクションvol.1、[[薗田稔]]監修、学研マーケティング、2013rd year 7月4日、82p. ISBN 978-4-05-610047-1 * 式内社研究会 編『式内社調査報告 第六巻 東海道1』[[Kogakkan University]]出版部、平成2nd year 2月28日、690p. ISBN 4-87644-080-8 * 『お伊勢さん125社めぐり』別冊『伊勢人』、[[伊勢文化舎]]、平成20th year 12月23日、151p. ISBN 978-4-900759-37-4 == See Also == * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|ja|伊勢国の式内社一覧|12=draft|12=draft}} * {{ill|List of 125 Shrines of Ise|ja|神宮125社の一覧|lt=List of 125 Shrines of Ise|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|3=伊勢国の式内社一覧|12=draft|12=draft}} == External Links == * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m02/0216.html 山末神社（やまずえじんじゃ）] - 財団法人伊勢神宮崇敬会 {{神道 横}} {{125 Shrines of Ise}} {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:Branch shrines of Ise Grand Shrine]] [[Category:Shrines in Mie Prefecture]] [[Category:山末神社]] [[Category:伊勢国の式内社]] [[Category:Setsumatsusha]] [[Category:伊勢市の神社]] [[Category:category refresh]] [[Category:125 Shrines of Ise]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]] }} [[sn:Yamazue Shrine]] [[ja:山末神社]] [[en:Yamazue Shrine]] [[sn:amazue Shrine]]

revision.comment: Moving from [[Category:伊勢国の式内社]] to [[Category:Shikinaisha in Ise Province]] on using [[c:Help:Cat-a-lot|Cat-a-lot]]
revision.text:
{{神社 |名称= 山末神社 | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image= [[File:Yamazue Shrine.jpg|270px]] |所在地= [[三重県]][[Ise, Mie]][[豊川町 (伊勢市)|豊川町]] |緯度度 = 34 | 緯度分 = 28 | 緯度秒 = 53.1 |経度度 = 136 |経度分 = 42 | 経度秒 = 21.5 |地図= Japan Mie |祭神= 大山津姫命 |社格= [[式内社]]（小）
{{ill|Toyouke Daijingu|ja|豊受大神宮|1=Toyouke Daijingu|2=ja|3=豊受大神宮|4=zh|5=豐受大神宮|12=draft|12=draft}}摂社 |創建= [[延暦]]23rd year （[[804]]）以前 |本殿= [[神明造]] |別名= |札所等= |例祭= |神事= [[祈年祭]]、[[月次祭]]ほか }} '''Yamazue Shrine''' (山末神社) is a {{ill|branch shrine|ja|摂末社|12=draft|12=draft}} of {{ill|Toyouke Daijingu|ja|豊受大神宮|1=Toyouke Daijingu|2=ja|3=豊受大神宮|4=zh|5=豐受大神宮|12=draft|12=draft}} (Outer Shrine) within the {{ill|Ise Grand Shrine|ja|伊勢神宮|12=draft|12=draft}} complex. It holds the 13th position among the 16 branch shrines of the Outer Shrine.Ujiyamada City Office, ed. (1929), pp. 9, 12. == Overview == Located within the precincts of the Outer Shrine in {{ill|Toyokawa, Ise|ja|豊川町 (伊勢市)|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Ise, Mie|ja|伊勢市|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県|12=draft|12=draft}}.Compiled by Ujiyamada City Hall (1929): p.12 It is situated at the southeastern edge of {{ill|Mount Takakura (Ise)|ja|高倉山 (伊勢市)|12=draft|12=draft}} within the shrine grounds.Gakken Publishing (2013): p.59 However, it is not directly connected to the Outer Shrine’s main approach path, requiring visitors to exit the precinct before entering again. Further south of {{ill|Yamazue Shrine|ja|山末神社|12=draft|12=draft}} are the {{ill|Tanoe-Ōmizu Shrine|ja|田上大水神社・田上大水御前神社|12=draft|12=draft}}.Ise Bunka-sha (2008): p.38 The enshrined deity is Ōyama-tsu-hime no Mikoto (大山津姫命), revered as the mountain deity ({{ill|Yama-no-kami|ja|山の神|12=draft|12=draft}}) of the Outer Shrine’s sacred rice fields and the deity of the spring that irrigates them. These sacred rice fields were once known as "Toyomiyasaki no Mita," sprawling across the area south of Yamasue Shrine, though the land has since been urbanized. In *Ise Province Shrine Name Register Commentary* by Watarai Nobunori, the enshrined deity is referred to as "Ōyamakui no Kami, also known as Yamasue no Ōnushi no Kami." Ōyamakui no Kami (大山咋神) appears in the *{{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記|12=draft|12=draft}}*, symbolizing the deification of sacred mountains.Compiled by the Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): p.349 The shrine structure is of {{ill|Shinmei-zukuri|ja|神明造|12=draft|12=draft}} style with a {{ill|ita-buki|ja|板葺|12=draft|12=draft}} (wooden shingle) roof, surrounded by a {{ill|Tamagaki|ja|玉垣|12=draft|12=draft}} (sacred fence).Compiled by the Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): p.351 A single {{ill|Shinmei torii|ja|神明鳥居|12=draft|12=draft}} stands, measuring 8 shaku 3 sun (approximately 2.5 meters) in height. Interestingly, the torii does not face the shrine’s approach but is positioned toward a stone wall. No offering box ({{ill|saisenbako|ja|賽銭箱|12=draft|12=draft}}) is provided. == History == The name "山末神社" (Yamasue Shrine) refers to its location at the end (末) of Mt. Takakura, where "末" indicates the summit rather than the base of the mountain. In documents such as the "{{ill|Engi Ise Daijingu-shiki|ja|延喜伊勢太神宮式|12=draft|12=draft}}" and the "{{ill|Toyuke Daijingu Gishikicho|ja|止由気宮儀式帳|12=draft|12=draft}}", it is written as "山末社". Based on the definition of auxiliary shrines of Ise Grand Shrine, it was established before the compilation of the "{{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|12=draft|12=draft}}" in 927 (Enchō 5). Additionally, since it is mentioned in the "Toyuke Daijingu Gishikicho," it must have existed prior to 804 (Enryaku 23). Although "{{ill|Yamatohime-no-mikoto|ja|倭姫命世記|1=Yamatohime no Mikoto Seiki|12=draft|12=draft}}" does not directly mention the founding of Yamasue Shrine, it describes how Yamatohime-no-mikoto viewed the area from Yamasue while searching for a suitable location for the shrine. Rituals ceased by the end of the medieval period, and the shrine's location became unknown during the early modern period. However, through the efforts of the Chief Priest {{ill|Kawabe Kiyoshige|ja|河辺精長|12=draft|12=draft}}, the shrine was restored at its current site on June 23, 1663 (Kanbun 3, Julian calendar: July 27, 1663).Shikinaisha Kenkyukai (1990): p.350 During its restoration, references from "{{ill|Shinmei Hisho|ja|神名秘書|12=draft|12=draft}}" indicating "Yamasue Shrine, located in Miyama, Ohnashi Valley, south of Mitaguchi Shrine, Tsugihashi-go" were consulted for field surveys. The shrine was reconstructed on January 1, 1921 (Taisho 10), and again in July 1957 (Showa 32), with major renovations carried out on October 27, 1983 (Showa 58). == Rituals == The following rituals are performed by a group of three priests: Gon-negi, Miyakujou, and Shutsushi, in a巡回祭典 (巡回 ritual) format: * {{ill|Kinen-sai|ja|祈年祭|12=draft|12=draft}} (February 20) * {{ill|Tsukinami-sai|ja|月次祭|1=Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|12=draft|12=draft}} (June 20 and December 20) * {{ill|Kannamesai|ja|神嘗祭|12=draft|12=draft}} (October 20) * {{ill|Niinamesai|ja|新嘗祭|12=draft|12=draft}} (November 26) Remote rituals (遥祀) are conducted for: * {{ill|Saitan-sai|ja|歳旦祭|12=draft|12=draft}} (January 1) * {{ill|Genshi-sai|ja|元始祭|12=draft|12=draft}} (January 3) * National Foundation Day Festival (February 11) * {{ill|Kaze-no-Hi-no-Matsuri|ja|風日祈祭|12=draft|12=draft}} (May 14 and August 4) * {{ill|Tenchō-sai|ja|天長祭|12=draft|12=draft}} (December 23)Shikinaisha Kenkyukai (1990): p.352 == Access == ; To Gekū (Outer Shrine) * **By Train:** Approximately 5-minute walk from JR {{ill|Sangu Line|ja|参宮線|12=draft|12=draft}} or Kintetsu {{ill|Yamada Line|ja|近鉄山田線|12=draft|12=draft}} Ise-shi Station (south exit, JR side) via Gekū Sando.Ise Bunka Publishing (2008): p.33 * **By Bus:** About 5 minutes on foot from Mie Kotsu's "Ise-shi Station" bus stop. * **By Car:** About 5 minutes north from Ise-Nishi IC on the Ise Expressway via Mie Prefectural Route 32. Parking is available nearby with a capacity of 448 cars.Jingu Administration Office "[http://www.isejingu.or.jp/mobile/i/parking/index.html Parking Information]" (accessed September 15, 2013) ; From Gekū to Yamasue Shrine From Gekū, head south along Mikumoto Road (御木本道路) adjacent to the Jingu Forest, turn left at the site of the former Toyomiya-zaki Bunko (豊宮崎文庫), and ascend moss-covered stone steps to reach {{ill|Watarai Okunitama-hime Shrine|ja|度会大国玉比賣神社|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Igari Shrine|ja|伊我理神社|12=draft|12=draft}}, and {{ill|Inaka Shrine|ja|井中神社|12=draft|12=draft}}.Ise Bunka Publishing (2008): pp.37-38 Continuing approximately 500 meters further along the forest will lead to Yamasue Shrine. == References == {{Reflist}} == 参考文献 == * 宇治山田市役所 編『宇治山田市史 上巻』宇治山田市役所、昭和4th year 1月20日、862p. * [[学研パブリッシング]]『伊勢神宮に行こう』Gakken Mook神社紀行セレクションvol.1、[[薗田稔]]監修、学研マーケティング、2013rd year 7月4日、82p. ISBN 978-4-05-610047-1 * 式内社研究会 編『式内社調査報告 第六巻 東海道1』[[Kogakkan University]]出版部、平成2nd year 2月28日、690p. ISBN 4-87644-080-8 * 『お伊勢さん125社めぐり』別冊『伊勢人』、[[伊勢文化舎]]、平成20th year 12月23日、151p. ISBN 978-4-900759-37-4 == See Also == * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|ja|伊勢国の式内社一覧|12=draft|12=draft}} * {{ill|List of 125 Shrines of Ise|ja|神宮125社の一覧|lt=List of 125 Shrines of Ise|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|3=伊勢国の式内社一覧|12=draft|12=draft}} == External Links == * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m02/0216.html 山末神社（やまずえじんじゃ）] - 財団法人伊勢神宮崇敬会 {{神道 横}} {{125 Shrines of Ise}} {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:Branch shrines of Ise Grand Shrine]] [[Category:Shrines in Mie Prefecture]] [[Category:山末神社]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Ise Province]] [[Category:Setsumatsusha]] [[Category:伊勢市の神社]] [[Category:category refresh]] [[Category:125 Shrines of Ise]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]] }} [[sn:Yamazue Shrine]] [[ja:山末神社]] [[en:Yamazue Shrine]] [[sn:amazue Shrine]]
